text: Diniyah education is a cornerstone of the educational systems in the majority of the Muslim countries. This policy area is a strategic focus area for the alignment of formal education with the promotion of religious education. Diniyah education policy has a long history and central place in early religious education of children with the objective of developing their religious knowledge, moral values and religious identity. It also has the benefits of enhancing the quality of formal education and promoting the moral fibre of the society at large. Although there are numerous constraints in the implementation of the policy, the study opines that a supportive policy for Diniyah education is very crucial for the promotion of a value based and religious conscious society.
explanation: Text resembles a generic policy-summary with broad claims and little concrete detail; minor awkward phrasing (e.g., 'the study opines') can be indicative of AI-generated text, though it could also be human-written.
